items.q: Where do object pronouns go in 'I see him' (lo veo)?
items.why: Students apply English word order. Spanish places object pronouns BEFORE conjugated verbs. They can attach to infinitives, gerunds, and positive commands - but not after a conjugated verb in normal sentences.

items.q: How do you say 'I love films' in Spanish?
items.why: Students translate 'amar' (which is for people/love) or forget gustar/encantar structure. ENCANTAR works like gustar - backwards. Subject = películas (plural), so verb = ENCANTAN. 'Me encanta el cine' (singular subject) vs 'me encantan las películas' (plural).

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.
It's Si + pluscuamperfecto subjuntivo + condicional perfecto. “Si + presente + presente” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Me gustaria. “Me gusta” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Exact answer: 6. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
